description: Complete extraction of the Video Surveillance Systems section including parent/child questions, all components, OFCs, visibility logic, and scoring rules

questions.title: 1. Video Surveillance Systems (VSS)
questions.question_text: What type of video surveillance system do you have?

answer_options.label: Hybrid system (mix of digital and analog)

options_for_consideration.text: Explore the feasibility of installing a comprehensive video surveillance system onsite. Site-specific factors must be considered when selecting components that comprise a particular system.
Consult Reference Manual to Mitigate Potential Terrorist Attacks Against Buildings (FEMA 426/BIPS 06), available at https://www.dhs.gov/xlibrary/assets/st/st-bips-06.pdf, for more information.

visibility_logic.description: Controls visibility of child question VideoAnalytics

scoring.note: VideoSystemType itself has no components, so it doesn't contribute to standards met/lost
scoring.impact: Affects visibility of VideoAnalytics question, which does contribute to scoring

questions.title: Video Analytics Capabilities
questions.question_text: Does your system include video analytics capabilities?

answer_options.label: N/A
answer_options.note: N/A option is hidden for digital/hybrid systems (logic error prevention)

components.text: Motion Detection – automated alerts for movement in designated areas
Intrusion Detection – monitoring restricted areas for unauthorized entry
Object Left Behind Detection – alerting when items are placed and left unattended
People Counting – tracking the number of individuals in defined zones
Line Crossing Detection – alerts when people cross defined virtual boundaries
Loitering Detection – identifying individuals lingering in sensitive areas
Tailgating Detection – alerting when multiple people enter on single authorization
License Plate Recognition (LPR) – automated reading of vehicle license plates
Vehicle Analytics – vehicle counting and parking monitoring
Fire & Smoke Detection – early warning through video-based recognition
Heat Mapping – visualizing movement patterns for facility optimization
Facial Recognition – identifying individuals against watchlists (if legally compliant)

options_for_consideration.text: Consider upgrading to a digital/IP-based system to enable advanced video analytics capabilities
Evaluate the cost-benefit of implementing video analytics for your specific security requirements
Consult with security system integrators about available analytics solutions for your facility

visibility_logic.description: Controls visibility of components and OFCs based on VideoAnalytics answer

scoring.standards_met: Count of checked VideoAnalyticsComp1-12 when answer='yes'
scoring.standards_lost: Count of all 12 components when answer='no'
scoring.enhancement_opportunities: Count of unchecked components when answer='yes'

questions.title: 2. Video Surveillance Coverage
questions.question_text: Does your video surveillance system provide adequate coverage of critical areas?

components.text: Perimeter surveillance coverage
Entry and exit point monitoring
Parking area surveillance
Critical asset monitoring

options_for_consideration.text: Evaluate video surveillance coverage of critical areas to determine if it meets the facility's security requirements. Explore options to increase coverage as necessary.
Install cameras in areas such as the main entrance, at all vehicle access control points, guard booths, cashier booths, and elevator lobbies on every floor.

scoring.standards_met: Count of checked VideoCoverageComp1-4 when answer='yes'
scoring.standards_lost: Count of all 4 components when answer='no'
scoring.enhancement_opportunities: Count of unchecked components when answer='yes'

questions.title: 3. Video Surveillance Monitoring
questions.question_text: Does your facility use real-time monitoring for the video surveillance system?

components.text: Dedicated monitoring staff
24/7 monitoring coverage
Response procedures for suspicious activity

options_for_consideration.text: Implement real-time monitoring capabilities for video surveillance systems to enable immediate response to security incidents.

scoring.standards_met: Count of checked VideoMonitoringComp1-3 when answer='yes'
scoring.standards_lost: Count of all 3 components when answer='no'
scoring.enhancement_opportunities: Count of unchecked components when answer='yes'

questions.title: 4. Video Recording and Storage
questions.question_text: Does your video surveillance system record and store video footage?

components.text: Adequate recording duration and quality
Secure storage and backup systems
Video retention policies and procedures

options_for_consideration.text: Evaluate the need to record video surveillance footage, and select video recording and storage systems for the facility as appropriate.

scoring.standards_met: Count of checked VideoRecordingComp1-3 when answer='yes'
scoring.standards_lost: Count of all 3 components when answer='no'
scoring.enhancement_opportunities: Count of unchecked components when answer='yes'

toggleVideoAnalytics.description: Controls visibility of VideoAnalytics child question based on VideoSystemType selection

toggleVideoAnalyticsDetails.description: Controls visibility of VideoAnalytics components and OFCs based on VideoAnalytics answer
toggleVideoAnalyticsDetails.error_prevention: Includes logic to prevent N/A selection for digital/hybrid systems (lines 5744-5756)

toggleQuestionDetails.description: Standard function for all other questions (VideoCoverage, VideoMonitoring, VideoRecording)

scoring_logic.overview: Scoring for VSS questions follows the standard pattern

standards_met.description: Count checked components for questions answered 'yes'
standards_met.example: If VideoCoverage='yes' and VideoCoverageComp1 and Comp3 are checked → 2 standards met

standards_lost.description: Count all components for questions answered 'no'
standards_lost.example: If VideoMonitoring='no' and has 3 components → 3 standards lost

total_standards.description: Sum of all components for questions answered 'yes' or 'no'
total_standards.note: N/A responses don't contribute to total standards

enhancement_opportunities.example: If VideoRecording='yes' and has 3 components but only 1 is checked → 2 enhancement opportunities

html_structure.note: Questions are defined in HTML, not separate JS/JSON config files
html_structure.pattern:
{title}

{question_text}

fields.VideoAnalytics: Stored as string: 'yes', 'no', or 'na' (only if VideoSystemType is digital/hybrid)
